name: Palau (Republic of Palau / Beluu er a Belau)
summary: Palau is a Pacific Island common-law republic in Compact of Free Association with the United States (Compact 1994). Family-law framework operates under the Palau National Code Title 21 (Domestic Relations) drawing on US-derivative common-law substantive heritage via Compact framework and prior US Trust Territory administration. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by National Code Title 21 Chapters 1-7. The Supreme Court of Palau is the apex court for civil and criminal matters. Customary-law jurisdiction operates in parallel — Palau is structurally distinctive globally as the only state with constitutionally-recognised matrilineal customary-law framework (Belauan tradition). The Court of Common Pleas operates first-instance customary-law jurisdiction. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Supreme Court (Trial Division) and Court of Common Pleas. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. Palau is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the welfare-of-the-child principle. Palau is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Palau National Code Title 21 — Domestic Relations
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Domestic Relations Code codifying marriage, divorce, parental responsibility, and child custody framework. Chapters 1-7 govern parental responsibility and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Compact of Free Association
statutory_framework.relevance: Compact establishing Palau's sovereign status with defense and certain economic relationships with the US.

statutory_framework.title: Constitution — matrilineal customary-law
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitution constitutionally-recognising Belauan matrilineal customary-law framework.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Palau
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Court of Common Pleas
apex_courts.role: First-instance court for civil and customary-law matters.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health and Human Services, Palau
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Palauan family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands + UN-Trusteeship framework
key_developments.description: Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands (TTPI) established 18 July 1947 as UN Strategic Trusteeship under US administration following Japanese colonial framework (Nan'yō Guntō). Substantively distinctive Pacific UN-Trusteeship framework persisting through 1994 Compact. Foundational pre-independence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Palau National Code Title 21
key_developments.description: Federal Domestic Relations Code enacted codifying marriage, divorce, parental responsibility, and child custody framework. Chapters 1-7 govern parental responsibility and child custody. Foundational substantive-statutory framework persisting through subsequent constitutional-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution — matrilineal customary-law recognition + nuclear-free framework
key_developments.description: Constitution adopted 9 April 1979 (effective 1 January 1981) constitutionally-recognising Belauan matrilineal customary-law framework. Substantively distinctive globally only-state-with-constitutionally-recognised-matrilineal-customary-law framework. Nuclear-free Constitution Article XIII substantively distinctive globally only-nuclear-free-constitution framework. Foundational substantive-constitutional framework.

key_developments.title: 1986 Compact-rejection-referendum + 7-referendum-trajectory framework
key_developments.description: Compact-rejection-referendum series 1983, 1984, 1986, 1987, 1990, 1992, 1993 — substantively distinctive globally 7-Compact-referendum-trajectory due to constitutional-nuclear-free requirement requiring 75% threshold framework. Foundational pre-1994-Compact framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Compact of Free Association + independence
key_developments.description: Compact of Free Association with US ratified 9 November 1993, effective 1 October 1994; Palau achieved independence 1 October 1994 from US Trust Territory framework. Last UN Trust Territory to achieve independence. Substantively distinctive Pacific last-UN-Trust-Territory-independence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Palau UN membership + sovereign-state framework
key_developments.description: Palau admitted to UN 15 December 1994 — substantively distinctive Pacific UN-membership-via-Compact framework. Substantial sovereign-state framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and cross-border-jurisdiction-practice.

key_developments.title: Palau ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Palau ratified the UNCRC on 4 August 1995 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration applied through National Code Title 21 + Belauan matrilineal customary-law framework.

key_developments.title: Palau Sanctuary 2009 + climate-change-vulnerability framework
key_developments.description: Palau established world's first shark sanctuary 24 September 2009 — substantively distinctive Pacific environmental-conservation framework. Substantive Pacific Islands Forum climate-change-vulnerability framework substantively significant. Subsequent Palau National Marine Sanctuary 2015 framework.

key_developments.title: Compact of Free Association Renewed Agreements + Compact-II framework
key_developments.description: Compact of Free Association Renewed Agreements substantively reforming defense + economic framework through 2023-2024. Substantive 20-year renewal framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-cross-cutting-areas including expat-family contexts. China-Taiwan relations affecting Compact framework.

key_developments.title: Taiwan-recognition framework continuation + Ombudsman framework + Palau-US joint security framework
key_developments.description: Palau continuing Taiwan-recognition framework substantively distinctive Pacific Islands diplomatic-engagement framework (alongside Marshall Islands, Tuvalu). Office of the Ombudsman institutional framework addressing PA-related issues within National Code Title 21 + Belauan matrilineal customary-law framework. Palau-US joint security framework substantively significant Pacific institutional context. Substantively significant Pacific institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court of Palau + Court of Common Pleas — welfare-of-the-child substantive register + Whipps-Jr-presidency
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of Palau and Court of Common Pleas continue to develop welfare-of-the-child jurisprudence under National Code Title 21 + Belauan matrilineal customary-law framework in custody disputes within Surangel Whipps Jr. presidency since 21 January 2021. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption — within US-derivative-common-law + Belauan-matrilineal-customary-law framework.

structural_findings: Palau operates a US-derivative common-law framework with structurally distinctive Compact-of-Free-Association status — places Palau in the Compact-of-Free-Association cluster (with Marshall Islands and Micronesia) within the corpus.

Constitutional recognition of Belauan matrilineal customary-law is structurally distinctive globally — only state with constitutionally-recognised matrilineal customary-law framework within the corpus.
Newest Compact-of-Free-Association state (1994 ratification) within the corpus.
Non-Hague Convention status places Palau in the non-Hague Pacific cluster.

sources.title: Ministry of Health and Human Services

editorial_notes: Palau jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Trust-Territory-to-contemporary trajectory: 1947-Trust-Territory-of-the-Pacific-Islands-+-UN-Trusteeship + 1980-Palau-National-Code-Title-21 + 1981-Constitution-+-matrilineal-customary-law-+-nuclear-free + 1986-1986-Compact-rejection-referendum-+-7-referendum-trajectory + 1994-Compact-of-Free-Association-+-independence + 1995-Palau-UN-membership + 1995-UNCRC-ratification + 2009-Palau-Sanctuary-+-climate-change-vulnerability + 2023-Compact-Renewed-Agreements + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-Court-of-Common-Pleas-+-Whipps-Jr-presidency.

Common-law Pacific Compact-of-Free-Association state (National Code Title 21 + Compact 1994 + Constitution 1981 matrilineal customary-law + non-Hague). Only constitutionally-recognised matrilineal customary-law framework globally. Completes Compact-of-Free-Association cluster (MH+FM+PW).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive welfare-of-the-child analysis under National Code Title 21 + Belauan matrilineal customary-law framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Pacific-Island + common-law + US-derivative + Compact-of-Free-Association-cluster (with Marshall Islands, Micronesia) + only-state-with-constitutionally-recognised-matrilineal-customary-law-framework-globally-distinctive + only-nuclear-free-constitution-Article-XIII-globally-distinctive + 1986-Compact-rejection-referendum-7-referendum-trajectory-due-to-75-percent-threshold-nuclear-free + last-UN-Trust-Territory-to-achieve-independence-1994-distinctive + Palau-Sanctuary-2009-+-world's-first-shark-sanctuary + Compact-Renewed-Agreements-2023-+-China-Taiwan-relations + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
